message:
🐛 Fix broken terminal scrolling by re-dispatching clamped wheel events (#158)

The previous scroll handler blocked ALL wheel events and used
term.scrollLines() for manual scrollback. This broke scrolling entirely
because scrollLines() is a no-op when tmux is in alternate screen mode
(which it always is), and blocking all events prevented xterm.js from
forwarding mouse sequences to tmux.

Fix: Instead of swallowing events, intercept and re-dispatch synthetic
wheel events with clamped deltaY (max 150px) and reduced throttle
(80ms vs 120ms). The __clampedScroll flag prevents infinite loops.
xterm.js now receives the events and handles both its own scrollback
buffer AND tmux mouse-sequence forwarding naturally.

- Momentum 'rocket scroll' is still prevented via deltaY clamping + throttle
- Normal scrollback navigation works in both normal and alternate screen modes
- Added 7 vitest tests covering clamping, throttling, and passthrough behavior

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
